stories.title: Project scaffold
stories.description: Initialize Next.js 15 App Router project with Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, TypeScript strict, Inter + Noto Sans JP fonts
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists with next@15, react@19, tailwindcss, typescript dependencies
tsconfig.json has strict: true
tailwind.config.ts includes accent color #D32F2F
Inter and Noto Sans JP fonts configured in layout.tsx
npm run build succeeds with zero errors

stories.title: Common layout: Header, Footer, ToolLayout
stories.description: Shared navigation (PC: logo left, About/Contact right; mobile: hamburger), footer (PP, terms, copyright), ToolLayout wrapper for tool pages
acceptanceCriteria: src/components/Header.tsx renders logo, About link, Contact link on desktop, hamburger on mobile
src/components/Footer.tsx renders privacy, terms links and copyright 2026
src/components/tools/ToolLayout.tsx exists with back-to-list link, title, description, disclaimer slots
Root layout.tsx includes Header and Footer
Navigation links point to correct paths (/, /about, /contact, /privacy, /terms)

stories.title: Tools metadata (tools.json)
stories.description: Create src/data/tools.json with all 11 tools: slug, name, description, category
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/tools.json contains 11 tool entries
Each entry has slug, name, description, category fields
Slugs match PRD: disability-employment, cbm-calculator, overtime-36, concrete-volume, 106wall, utilization-rate, man-day, recruitment-cost, shift-labor-cost, building-area, depreciation
Categories match PRD: 人事・労務, 物流, 建設・土木, 制度変更, 経理, 不動産, 飲食・小売

stories.title: Top page: tool grid + category filter + UGA request
stories.description: Homepage with UGA request input, category filter buttons, 3-column tool card grid (no images)
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/page.tsx renders UGA request input with placeholder 'どんなツールが欲しいですか？'
Note under input: '入力内容はツール開発の参考に使用します'
Category filter buttons for all 7 categories
Tool cards displayed in 3-column grid on desktop, same-size cards, no images
Clicking a tool card navigates to /tools/[slug]
Category filter filters the displayed tools

stories.title: Static pages: About, Contact, Privacy, Terms
acceptanceCriteria: src/app/about/page.tsx renders About page in Japanese
src/app/contact/page.tsx has form with name, email, message fields
src/app/privacy/page.tsx renders privacy policy in Japanese
src/app/terms/page.tsx renders terms of service in Japanese
All pages use consistent layout with Header/Footer

stories.title: Tools 1-4: disability-employment, cbm-calculator, overtime-36, concrete-volume
stories.description: Implement first 4 tools per PRD spec using ToolLayout
acceptanceCriteria: disability-employment: input employees -> outputs required hires (ceil(n*0.027)), shortfall, levy (50000/person/month)
cbm-calculator: input L/W/H(cm) + qty -> outputs CBM, sai, volume weight (air/sea/truck)
overtime-36: input 12 months of overtime -> outputs diff from 45h/month and 360h/year limits, highlights exceeded months
concrete-volume: input area(m2) + thickness(cm) -> outputs volume(m3), +10% margin, mixer truck count (4.25m3/truck)
All 4 use ToolLayout with title, description, input, calculate button, results, formula explanation, disclaimer

stories.title: Tools 5-8: 106wall, utilization-rate, man-day, recruitment-cost
acceptanceCriteria: 106wall: input hourly wage + weekly hours -> outputs social insurance eligibility, annual income, estimated take-home change
utilization-rate: input planned time, actual time, production count, defect count -> outputs utilization %, defect %, yield %
man-day: input people, days, optional unit price -> outputs man-days, estimated cost
recruitment-cost: input media cost, personnel cost, referral fees, applications, hires -> outputs cost-per-hire, cost-per-application
All 4 use ToolLayout pattern

stories.title: Tools 9-11: shift-labor-cost, building-area, depreciation
stories.description: Implement final 3 tools per PRD spec
acceptanceCriteria: shift-labor-cost: input hourly wage, people, hours/day, days -> outputs daily/weekly/monthly labor cost
building-area: input land area(m2), FAR(%), BCR(%) -> outputs max building area, max total floor area
depreciation: input completion year/month, structure type (wood/RC/steel) -> outputs building age, statutory useful life, remaining useful life, depreciation rate
All 3 use ToolLayout pattern

stories.title: Build + typecheck pass
stories.description: Ensure the entire project builds and typechecks cleanly
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds with exit code 0
npx tsc --noEmit succeeds with exit code 0
npx biome check . reports no errors (or biome is configured and passes)
